papers.title: An RNA condensate model for the origin of life
papers.abstract: The RNA World hypothesis predicts that life originated from self-replicating RNA molecules. We present a model where RNA condensates provide the compartmentalization necessary for early replication, addressing key challenges in origin of life scenarios.

papers.title: Earth, a planetary PCR machine to create life, or the brief history of a tRNA
papers.abstract: About 4 billion years ago, the Earth probably fulfilled the environmental conditions necessary to favour the transition from primitive chemistry to life. Based on a theoretical hairpin duplication mechanism, we propose Earth acted as a planetary PCR machine.

papers.title: Stability distillation hypothesis for the origin of life
papers.abstract: Through in-depth thinking and reasoning about the conditions required for cells to maintain unchanged material distribution, we conclude that life metabolic reactions require high information content, proposing a stability distillation mechanism.

papers.title: Directionality Theory and the Origin of Life
papers.abstract: The origin of cellular life is analyzed through directionality theory, connecting thermodynamic principles with evolutionary dynamics in early life forms.

papers.title: Thermodynamics and the Origin of Life
papers.abstract: Modern developments in nonequilibrium thermodynamics have significant implications for the origins of life, particularly regarding energy dissipation and self-organization.

papers.title: Origin of Chirality in the Molecules of Life
papers.abstract: Molecular chirality is inherent to biology and cellular chemistry. We analyze the origin of enantiomeric selectivity from thermodynamic and symmetry-breaking perspectives.

papers.title: Emergence of life in an inflationary universe
papers.abstract: Abiotic emergence of ordered information stored in the form of RNA is an important unresolved problem. We explore how inflationary cosmology may provide conditions favorable for RNA emergence.

papers.title: Horizontal transfer between loose compartments stabilizes replication of fragmented ribozymes
papers.abstract: The emergence of replicases that can replicate themselves is a central issue. We show how horizontal transfer between compartments stabilizes fragmented ribozyme replication.

papers.title: Evolvability tradeoffs in emergent digital replicators
papers.abstract: The role of historical contingency in the origin of self-replicating systems is explored through digital evolution experiments.

papers.title: Primordial RNA Replication and Applications in PCR Technology
papers.abstract: The emergence of self-replicating RNA molecules and their connection to modern PCR technology provides insights into primordial replication mechanisms.

papers.title: A formal model of autocatalytic sets emerging in an RNA replicator system
papers.abstract: Autocatalytic sets played an important role in the origin of life. We present a formal model of their emergence in RNA replicator systems.

papers.title: Thermosynthesis as energy source for the RNA World: a new model for the origin of life
papers.abstract: We propose thermosynthesis as a viable energy source for early RNA World scenarios, providing a mechanism for non-enzymatic replication.

keyInsights: RNA condensate models address compartmentalization challenges in origin of life scenarios
Planetary PCR mechanism proposes Earth's thermal cycles drove early replication
Stability distillation hypothesis links information content to metabolic stability
Directionality theory connects thermodynamics with early evolutionary dynamics
Nonequilibrium thermodynamics provides framework for self-organization in origin scenarios
Horizontal transfer between compartments stabilizes fragmented replicator systems
Autocatalytic sets formal models show emergence pathways for early replicators
Thermosynthesis offers alternative energy mechanisms for RNA World
chronosRelevance: 直接支持生命起源的热力学理论，特别是 RNA 凝聚体模型与 ITLCT 的耗散结构理论高度契合；稳定性蒸馏假说支持信息 - 热力学耦合；自催化集模型为从非生命到生命的相变提供数学框架
